an10.76:1.1: “Monks, if these three things were not to be found in the world, the Tathāgata—the worthy one, rightly self-awakened—would not appear in the world, and the Dhamma & Vinaya proclaimed by the Tathāgata would not shine in the world.
an10.76:1.4: If these three things were not to be found in the world, the Tathāgata—the worthy one, rightly self-awakened—would not appear in the world, and the Dhamma & Vinaya proclaimed by the Tathāgata would not shine in the world.
an10.76:1.5: But because these three things are to be found in the world, the Tathāgata—the worthy one, rightly self-awakened—has appeared in the world, and the Dhamma & Vinaya proclaimed by the Tathāgata shines in the world.”
an10.76:2.1: “Without abandoning three things, one is incapable of abandoning birth, abandoning aging, abandoning death.
an10.76:2.4: Without abandoning these three things, one is incapable of abandoning birth, abandoning aging, abandoning death.”
an10.76:3.1: “Without abandoning three things, one is incapable of abandoning passion, abandoning aversion, abandoning delusion.
an10.76:3.3: Self-identification views, uncertainty, & grasping at habits & practices:
an10.76:3.4: Without abandoning these three things, one is incapable of abandoning passion, abandoning aversion, abandoning delusion.”
an10.76:4.1: “Without abandoning three things, one is incapable of abandoning self-identification views, abandoning uncertainty, abandoning grasping at habits & practices.
an10.76:4.3: Inappropriate attention, following a wrong path, & slowness of awareness…”
an10.76:5.1: “Without abandoning three things, one is incapable of abandoning inappropriate attention, abandoning the following of a wrong path, abandoning slowness of awareness.
an10.76:6.1: “Without abandoning three things, one is incapable of abandoning muddled truth, abandoning unalertness, abandoning scattered awareness.
an10.76:6.3: A lack of desire to see the noble ones, a lack of desire to hear the noble Dhamma, & a mind bent on criticism…”
an10.76:7.1: “Without abandoning three things, one is incapable of abandoning a lack of desire to see the noble ones, abandoning a lack of desire to hear the noble Dhamma, abandoning a mind bent on criticism.
an10.76:7.3: Restlessness, a lack of restraint, & poor virtue …”
an10.76:8.1: “Without abandoning three things, one is incapable of abandoning restlessness, abandoning a lack of restraint, abandoning poor virtue.
an10.76:8.3: A lack of conviction, stinginess, & laziness…”
an10.76:9.1: “Without abandoning three things, one is incapable of abandoning a lack of conviction, abandoning stinginess, abandoning laziness.
an10.76:9.3: Apathy, being hard to correct, & evil friendship…”
an10.76:10.1: “Without abandoning three things, one is incapable of abandoning apathy, being hard to correct, & evil friendship.
an10.76:10.3: Being shameless, lacking compunction, & being heedless.
an10.76:10.4: Without abandoning these three things, one is incapable of abandoning apathy, being hard to correct, & evil friendship.”
an10.76:11.1: “Monks, being shameless & lacking compunction, one is heedless.
an10.76:11.2: “Being heedless, one is incapable of abandoning apathy, being hard to correct, & evil friendship.
an10.76:11.3: “Having evil friendship, one is incapable of abandoning a lack of conviction, stinginess, & laziness.
an10.76:11.4: “Being lazy, one is incapable of abandoning restlessness, a lack of restraint, & poor virtue.
an10.76:11.5: “Being unvirtuous, one is incapable of abandoning a lack of desire to see the noble ones, a lack of desire to hear the noble Dhamma, a mind bent on criticism.
an10.76:11.6: “Having a mind bent on criticism, one is incapable of abandoning muddled truth, unalertness, & scattered awareness.
an10.76:11.7: “Having scattered awareness, one is incapable of abandoning inappropriate attention, the following of a wrong path, & slowness of awareness.
an10.76:11.8: “Having slow awareness, one is incapable of abandoning self-identification, uncertainty, & grasping at habits & practices.
an10.76:11.9: “Being uncertain, one is incapable of abandoning passion, aversion, & delusion.
an10.76:11.10: “Without abandoning passion, aversion, & delusion, one is incapable of abandoning birth, abandoning aging, abandoning death.”
an10.76:12.1: “Abandoning three things, one is capable of abandoning birth, abandoning aging, abandoning death.
an10.76:12.4: Abandoning these three things, one is capable of abandoning birth, abandoning aging, abandoning death.”
an10.76:13.1: “Abandoning three things, one is capable of abandoning passion, abandoning aversion, abandoning delusion.
an10.76:13.3: Self-identification views, uncertainty, & grasping at habits & practices:
an10.76:13.4: Abandoning these three things, one is capable of abandoning passion, abandoning aversion, abandoning delusion.”
an10.76:14.1: “Abandoning three things, one is capable of abandoning self-identification views, abandoning uncertainty, abandoning grasping at habits & practices.
an10.76:14.3: Inappropriate attention, following a wrong path, & slowness of awareness…”
an10.76:15.1: “Abandoning three things, one is capable of abandoning inappropriate attention, abandoning the following of a wrong path, abandoning slowness of awareness.
an10.76:16.1: “Abandoning three things, one is capable of abandoning muddled truth, abandoning unalertness, abandoning scattered awareness.
an10.76:16.3: A lack of desire to see the noble ones, a lack of desire to hear the noble Dhamma, & a mind bent on criticism…”
an10.76:17.1: “Abandoning three things, one is capable of abandoning a lack of desire to see the noble ones, abandoning a lack of desire to hear the noble Dhamma, abandoning a mind bent on criticism.
an10.76:17.3: Restlessness, a lack of restraint, & poor virtue …”
an10.76:18.1: “Abandoning three things, one is capable of abandoning restlessness, abandoning a lack of restraint, abandoning poor virtue.
an10.76:18.3: A lack of conviction, stinginess, & laziness…”
an10.76:19.1: “Abandoning three things, one is capable of abandoning a lack of conviction, abandoning stinginess, abandoning laziness.
an10.76:19.3: Apathy, being hard to correct, & evil friendship…”
an10.76:20.1: “Abandoning three things, one is capable of abandoning apathy, being hard to correct, & evil friendship.
an10.76:20.3: Being shameless, lacking compunction, & being heedless.
an10.76:20.4: Abandoning these three things, one is capable of abandoning apathy, being hard to correct, & evil friendship.”
an10.76:21.1: “Monks, having a sense of shame & having a sense of compunction, one is heedful.
an10.76:21.2: “Being heedful, one is capable of abandoning apathy, being hard to correct, & evil friendship.
an10.76:21.3: “Having admirable friendship, one is capable of abandoning a lack of conviction, stinginess, & laziness.
an10.76:21.4: “Having aroused persistence, one is capable of abandoning restlessness, a lack of restraint, & poor virtue.
an10.76:21.5: “Being virtuous, one is capable of abandoning a lack of desire to see the noble ones, a lack of desire to hear the noble Dhamma, a mind bent on criticism.
an10.76:21.6: “Having a mind not bent on criticism, one is capable of abandoning muddled truth, unalertness, & scattered awareness.
an10.76:21.7: “Having unscattered awareness, one is capable of abandoning inappropriate attention, the following of a wrong path, & slowness of awareness.
an10.76:21.8: “Having quick awareness, one is capable of abandoning self-identification, uncertainty, & grasping at habits & practices.
an10.76:21.9: “Being without uncertainty, one is capable of abandoning passion, aversion, & delusion.
an10.76:21.10: “Abandoning passion, aversion, & delusion, one is capable of abandoning birth, abandoning aging, abandoning death.”
